ConditionTab.Label: Condition
ConditionTab.Heading: Active Effect condition
ConditionTab.Hint: Use JavaScript. This Active Effect is applied only when the script returns true.
ConditionTab.CompatibilityHint: This condition came from DAE. SC Conditional AE is adapting it automatically.
ConditionTab.Variables: Available variables: effect, actor, targetActor, item, origin, originActor, user, rollData, source, getProperty, hasProperty, deepClone, game.

Evaluation.Heading: Current evaluation
Evaluation.Empty: No condition configured. The effect is available.
Evaluation.True: The condition currently resolves to true. The effect is available.
Evaluation.False: The condition currently resolves to false. The effect is suppressed.
Evaluation.Error: The condition threw an error while being evaluated.

ConditionTab.BadgeLabelHint: Single-line label shown on inactive effects. Leave blank to hide.
ConditionTab.ApplyBehaviorUpdateHint: Reapplies the current Active Effect on the target instead of creating a new one.
ConditionTab.ApplyBehaviorDuplicateHint: Adds a new Active Effect instead of updating the existing one.
ConditionTab.ApplyBehaviorDaeHint: Uses DAE's Stackable setting to decide whether the effect stacks.

MacroChange.Name: Macro to Execute
MacroChange.Description: Execute a world macro when this Active Effect is applied or removed. Value format: macro name or UUID, followed by optional arguments.

FormulaChange.DialogHint: Confirm or edit the formula to roll for this Active Effect.
FormulaChange.ChatCardHint: This effect's formulas are ready to roll.
FormulaChange.ChatCardIntroCondition: The condition for {effect} has been met.
FormulaChange.ChatCardPrompt: Do you want to roll now?
FormulaChange.ChatCardReasonActivation: {effect} is active, and its formulas can be rolled now.
FormulaChange.ChatCardReasonCondition: {effect} is ready, and its formulas can be rolled now.
FormulaChange.ColumnHint: Formula rolled when this Active Effect is activated.

EnableFormulaChanges.Name: Enable formula column
EnableFormulaChanges.Hint: Adds the Formula column to Active Effect changes and rolls formulas when effects are activated.

UseFormulaChatCard.Name: Post formula roll chat card
UseFormulaChatCard.Hint: Whenever a conditional effect becomes available, or when a formula-backed Active Effect is activated, post a chat card so the roll can be triggered from chat instead of immediately.

ShowConditionTab.Name: Show condition tab
ShowConditionTab.Hint: Adds the Condition tab to Active Effect configuration sheets.
ShowConditionTab.DaeNotice: If you use Dynamic Active Effects (DAE) with the condition tab enabled, a libWrapper conflict warning may appear in the browser console. This is expected — the module intentionally blocks effect application when a condition is not met, which also skips DAE's wrapper for those effects. You can safely ignore this warning.

DebugLogging.Name: Enable debug logging
DebugLogging.Hint: Logs condition evaluation, suppression refreshes, and activation transitions to the browser console.

SupportMenu.Name: Support the developer
SupportMenu.Label: Patreon support
SupportMenu.Hint: Support Shattered Codex development on Patreon.

DocumentationMenu.Name: Documentation
DocumentationMenu.Label: Open wiki
DocumentationMenu.Hint: Open the SC - Conditional AE documentation wiki.

ModuleSettingsMenu.Name: Module settings
ModuleSettingsMenu.Label: Open settings
ModuleSettingsMenu.Hint: Open the SC - Conditional AE configuration window.

App.Title: SC - Conditional AE Settings
App.Intro: Configure conditional Active Effect behavior, formula support, and module diagnostics.

WorldSection.Title: Effect behavior
WorldSection.Description: World-level settings that control condition tabs, formula changes, and chat-card behavior.
WorldSection.PermissionNote: Only a GM can change the settings in this section.

ClientSection.Title: Diagnostics
ClientSection.Description: Client-side settings for troubleshooting and local browser logging.

App.DaeWarning: This libWrapper message is only a warning. When DAE is active, both modules touch the same Active Effect pipeline, so the browser console may report a potential conflict even when behavior is working normally.
